title: Luxury is a standard, not a zip code
dek: The word luxury in your title is a promise about how you operate, not about the buildings you tour.
heroAlt: Luxury is a standard, not a zip code

blocks.html: Plenty of agents show expensive apartments. Far fewer are luxury agents. The difference has nothing to do with the rent roll and everything to do with the experience surrounding it: the preparation, the communication, the sense the client gets that someone competent is holding the entire process.

High-end clients are pattern readers. They notice whether you confirmed the appointment, whether you knew the answer or performed knowing it, whether the follow-up arrived when you said it would. Each detail is small. Together they are the product, because the apartments themselves the client could find alone.

blocks.text: In luxury, the service is the product. The apartment is just the venue.

items.title: Preparation is visible.
items.html: Route planned, units pre-walked when possible, a one-page brief on each building. Clients can tell prepared from winging it in ninety seconds.

items.title: Communication has a rhythm.
items.html: Responses inside agreed windows, updates before they are requested. Silence is the only true luxury killer.

items.title: The truth is the brand.
items.html: Steering a client away from a wrong unit, on record, is the most expensive-feeling thing you can do, and it costs nothing.

blocks.html: Write your personal service standard as five short promises. Print it, keep it visible, and audit yourself against it after every closing for a month.
Standards hold better with witnesses. The Elite Leasing Club is weekly coaching and a room of agents holding the same bar.

blocks.lead: The Club holds the standard, week after week.
blocks.label: Join the Elite Leasing Club
